Bash("C:\Users\bryan\miniconda3\python.exe" -c "
import sqlite3
print(''Adding order_number column to orders table...'')
conn = sqlite3.connect(''ecommerce.db'')
cursor = conn.cursor()
try:
    cursor.execute(''ALTER TABLE orders ADD COLUMN order_number VARCHAR'')
    conn.commit()
    print(''[OK] Column added successfully'')
except sqlite3.OperationalError as e:
    if ''duplicate column name'' in str(e):
        print(''[INFO] Column already exists'')
    else:
        print(f''[ERROR] {e}'')
finally:
    conn.close()
")

Bash("C:\Users\bryan\miniconda3\python.exe" -c "
import sqlite3
print(''Creating index on order_number column...'')
conn = sqlite3.connect(''ecommerce.db'')
cursor = conn.cursor()
try:
    cursor.execute(''CREATE INDEX ix_orders_order_number ON orders (order_number)'')
    conn.commit()
    print(''[OK] Index created successfully'')
except sqlite3.OperationalError as e:
    if ''already exists'' in str(e):
        print(''[INFO] Index already exists'')
    else:
        print(f''[ERROR] {e}'')
finally:
    conn.close()
")

import requests
import time
time.sleep(3)
try:
    response = requests.get(''http://127.0.0.1:3000'', timeout=5)
    print(f''Frontend status: {response.status_code}'')
    if response.status_code == 200:
        print(''[OK] Frontend actualizado exitosamente'')
        
        # Verificar las órdenes que ahora deberían aparecer en Dashboard
        orders_response = requests.get(''http://localhost:8002/api/orders'')
        if orders_response.status_code == 200:
            orders = orders_response.json()
            
            # Simular la lógica de ordenamiento del frontend
            orders_with_numbers = [o for o in orders if o.get(''order_number'')]
            orders_without_numbers = [o for o in orders if not o.get(''order_number'')]
            
            # Ordenar por fecha (más reciente primero)
            orders_with_numbers.sort(key=lambda x: x[''date''], reverse=True)
            orders_without_numbers.sort(key=lambda x: x[''date''], reverse=True)
            
            # Combinar: primero con números, después sin números
            sorted_orders = orders_with_numbers + orders_without_numbers
            top_5 = sorted_orders[:5]
            
            print(''\nÓrdenes que aparecerán en Dashboard (Top 5):'')
            for i, order in enumerate(top_5, 1):
                order_display = order.get(''order_number'') or order.get(''id'')
                customer = order.get(''customer_name'')
                status = order.get(''status'')
                total = order.get(''total'', 0)
                print(f''{i}. {order_display} | {customer} | {status} | $total:,.0f'')
        else:
            print(''[ERROR] No se pudieron cargar las órdenes'')
    else:
        print(f''[WARNING] Frontend responde con status: {response.status_code}'')
except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
    print(f''[ERROR] Error al conectar con frontend: {e}'')
")

Bash("C:\Users\bryan\miniconda3\python.exe" -c "
import requests
import sqlite3

# Get all orders from API
response = requests.get(''http://localhost:8002/api/orders'')
if response.status_code == 200:
    orders = response.json()
    
    # Connect to database
    conn = sqlite3.connect(''backend/ecommerce.db'')
    cursor = conn.cursor()
    
    # Find orders without order numbers and assign them
    orders_without_numbers = [o for o in orders if not o.get(''order_number'')]
    
    print(f''Found {len(orders_without_numbers)} orders without order numbers'')
    
    # Get the highest existing order number
    cursor.execute(''SELECT order_number FROM orders WHERE order_number IS NOT NULL ORDER BY order_number DESC LIMIT 1'')
    result = cursor.fetchone()
    
    if result:
        last_number = int(result[0].split(''-'')[1])
    else:
        last_number = 0
    
    print(f''Last order number: ORD-{last_number:06d}'')
    
    # Update orders without numbers
    for i, order in enumerate(orders_without_numbers):
        new_number = f''ORD-{last_number + i + 1:06d}''
        cursor.execute(''UPDATE orders SET order_number = ? WHERE id = ?'', (new_number, order[''id'']))
        print(f''Updated order {order[""id""]} -> {new_number}'')
    
    conn.commit()
    conn.close()
    
    print(f''\nSuccessfully updated {len(orders_without_numbers)} orders with order numbers'')
else:
    print(f''Failed to get orders: {response.status_code}'')
")

import requests
import time
time.sleep(2)
try:
    response = requests.get(''http://127.0.0.1:3000'', timeout=5)
    print(f''Frontend status: {response.status_code}'')
    if response.status_code == 200:
        print(''[OK] Frontend funcionando correctamente'')
        
        # Check orders with order numbers
        orders_response = requests.get(''http://localhost:8002/api/orders'')
        if orders_response.status_code == 200:
            orders = orders_response.json()
            
            # Simulate the frontend sorting logic
            orders_with_numbers = [o for o in orders if o.get(''order_number'')]
            orders_without_numbers = [o for o in orders if not o.get(''order_number'')]
            
            # Sort by date (most recent first)
            orders_with_numbers.sort(key=lambda x: x[''date''], reverse=True)
            orders_without_numbers.sort(key=lambda x: x[''date''], reverse=True)
            
            # Combine: first with numbers, then without numbers
            sorted_orders = orders_with_numbers + orders_without_numbers
            top_5 = sorted_orders[:5]
            
            print(f''\nTotal orders: {len(orders)}'')
            print(f''Orders with numbers: {len(orders_with_numbers)}'')
            print(f''Orders without numbers: {len(orders_without_numbers)}'')
            
            print(''\nTop 5 orders that will appear in Dashboard:'')
            for i, order in enumerate(top_5, 1):
                order_display = order.get(''order_number'') or order.get(''id'')
                customer = order.get(''customer_name'')
                status = order.get(''status'')
                total = order.get(''total'', 0)
                print(f''{i}. {order_display} | {customer} | {status} | $total:,.0f'')
        else:
            print(''[ERROR] No se pudieron cargar las órdenes'')
    else:
        print(f''[WARNING] Frontend responde con status: {response.status_code}'')
except requests.exceptions.RequestException as e:
    print(f''[ERROR] Error al conectar con frontend: {e}'')
")

Bash("C:\Users\bryan\miniconda3\python.exe" -c "
import requests

# Test the Dashboard API endpoint that the frontend uses
orders_response = requests.get(''http://localhost:8002/api/orders'')
if orders_response.status_code == 200:
    orders = orders_response.json()
    print(''Dashboard ""Recent Orders"" will now show:'')
    
    # Mimic exact frontend logic from Dashboard.tsx
    orders_with_numbers = [o for o in orders if o.get(''order_number'')]
    orders_without_numbers = [o for o in orders if not o.get(''order_number'')]
    
    # Sort by date (most recent first)
    from datetime import datetime
    orders_with_numbers.sort(key=lambda x: datetime.fromisoformat(x[''date''].replace(''Z'', ''+00:00'')), reverse=True)
    orders_without_numbers.sort(key=lambda x: datetime.fromisoformat(x[''date''].replace(''Z'', ''+00:00'')), reverse=True)
    
    # Combine: first with numbers, then without numbers
    sorted_orders = orders_with_numbers + orders_without_numbers
    top_5 = sorted_orders[:5]
    
    for i, order in enumerate(top_5, 1):
        # This is the exact logic from Dashboard.tsx line 65: order.orderNumber || order.id
        display_value = order.get(''order_number'') or order.get(''id'')
        customer = order.get(''customer_name'')
        status = order.get(''status'')
        total = order.get(''total'', 0)
        print(f''{i}. {display_value} | {customer} | {status} | $total:,.0f'')
    
    print(f''\n[SUCCESS] All {len(orders)} orders now have order numbers!'')
    print(f''[SUCCESS] Dashboard will show order numbers instead of IDs'')
else:
    print(f''[ERROR] Could not load orders: {orders_response.status_code}'')
")

import requests

# Simple test
print(''[TEST] Simple auth test...'')
try:
    # Test register
    register_data = {
        ''email'': ''test-user@simple-test.com'',
        ''password'': ''test123'',
        ''client_name'': ''Simple Test Company''
    }
    
    response = requests.post(''http://localhost:8002/auth/register'', json=register_data)
    print(f''Register response: {response.status_code}'')
    
    if response.status_code == 200:
        auth_data = response.json()
        print(''[OK] Registration successful'')
        print(f''User: {auth_data[""user""][""email""]}'')
        print(f''Client: {auth_data[""client""][""name""]}'')
        
        # Test login
        login_data = {
            ''email'': ''test-user@simple-test.com'',
            ''password'': ''test123''
        }
        
        login_response = requests.post(''http://localhost:8002/auth/login'', json=login_data)
        print(f''Login response: {login_response.status_code}'')
        
        if login_response.status_code == 200:
            print(''[OK] Login successful'')
        else:
            print(f''[ERROR] Login failed: {login_response.text}'')
    else:
        print(f''[ERROR] Registration failed: {response.text}'')
        
except Exception as e:
    print(f''[ERROR] Test failed: {e}'')
")
